solution: 
```python
from collections import deque, defaultdict

class Solution:
    def findAllPeople(self, n: int, meetings: List[List[int]], firstPerson: int) -> List[int]:
        # Build time -> list of meetings
        time_map = defaultdict(list)
        for x, y, t in meetings:
            time_map[t].append((x, y))
        
        # Initialize known set
        known = {0, firstPerson}
        
        # Process meetings in increasing time order
        for t in sorted(time_map.keys()):
            # Build graph for this time
            graph = defaultdict(list)
            nodes = set()
            for x, y in time_map[t]:
                graph[x].append(y)
                graph[y].append(x)
                nodes.update([x, y])
            
            # BFS from all known nodes in this graph
            q = deque([p for p in nodes if p in known])
            while q:
                cur = q.popleft()
                for nei in graph[cur]:
                    if nei not in known:
                        known.add(nei)
                        q.append(nei)
        
        return list(known)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(M log M + N + M)
space complexity: O(N + M)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Group meetings by time, process each time slot in order. For each time, build a graph of meetings and perform BFS from all currently known people to propagate the secret. Sorting meetings by time dominates the complexity.
